description:
GOAL: Inline across module boundaries.

Status when authored: speculative. Listed Open in original fz-ul4.11.15
("Whole-program vs. per-fn inlining strategy").

## Why this is non-trivial

  fz's module system flattens to one Module at ir_lower::lower_program time
  (via resolve::flatten_modules in main.rs:167). So at IR time there IS no
  module boundary — every fn lives in one Module. This means cross-module
  inlining is ALREADY possible in fz-ul4.11.15.

  HOWEVER: if/when fz gains separate compilation (per-file AOT artifacts,
  cached IR, hot-reload of a single fn), cross-module inlining will
  invalidate the consumer artifact when the producer fn changes. That's
  what this ticket is for: a dependency-tracking mechanism so cached
  consumer artifacts know which producer fns they've inlined.

## Sketch

  - Persist per-CompiledModule an `inlined_callees: HashMap<FnId,
    Vec<CallerFnId>>` recording who inlined whom.
  - On reload of a producer fn, invalidate consumer artifacts that inlined
    its previous version.
  - Likely requires content-hashing FnIrs.

## When to start

  Only after separate compilation / IR caching exists. No dep on .11.15
  beyond .15 having landed.

## Acceptance

  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.26.
title: Cross-module inlining (cache invalidation)
